pattern: Since the subarray may be freely rearranged, checking 'can this become arithmetic' reduces to sorting the slice and checking that consecutive gaps are all equal.
hints: The subarray can be reordered however you like, so the only thing that matters is the multiset of values in it, not their original positions.
Sort each queried slice; a multiset can form an arithmetic sequence exactly when the sorted version has a constant gap between consecutive values.
Handle each query independently by slicing nums[l[i]..r[i]], sorting that slice, and scanning it once to confirm every consecutive gap matches the first gap.

edge_inputs.description: mix of a true arithmetic slice, a false one, and a longer true one
several negative-number slices of mixed results
minimum-length slice of exactly two elements is always arithmetic
unsorted slice that only becomes arithmetic after reordering
sorted slice with an uneven gap in the middle
constant slice, gap of zero throughout
whole array already sorted and arithmetic

stress.description: random array and many random l/r query pairs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 500))
    nums = [rng.randint(-100000, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    m = min(500, n)
    l, r = [], []
    for _ in range(m):
        lo = rng.randint(0, n - 2)
        hi = rng.randint(lo + 1, n - 1)
        l.append(lo)
        r.append(hi)
    return [nums, l, r]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def checkArithmeticSubarrays(self, nums, l, r):
        result = []
        for lo, hi in zip(l, r):
            sub = nums[lo:hi + 1]
            n = len(sub)
            if n < 2:
                result.append(True)
                continue
            mn, mx = min(sub), max(sub)
            if mx == mn:
                result.append(True)
                continue
            if (mx - mn) % (n - 1) != 0:
                result.append(False)
                continue
            diff = (mx - mn) // (n - 1)
            seen = set(sub)
            ok = len(seen) == n and all((mn + k * diff) in seen for k in range(n))
            result.append(ok)
        return result
